rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains methodological claims (mixed-effects model, counterbalancing, coding agreement) and inferential conclusions that need logical consistency checks between design, analysis, and interpretation.
rationale.R1.3: Key claims—numeric phrases yield higher trust ratings, free-text justifications support an 'authoritative' theme—must be checked against whether the reported evidence (model output, inter-rater agreement) actually licenses them.
rationale.R1.6: The prose contains run-on sentences, tense inconsistencies (e.g., 'completes' vs. 'were asked'), and unclear antecedents; 'check this' plausibly targets surface writing quality.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on writing quality (grammar, tense consistency, sentence structure), or on the methodological and logical soundness of the study design and reported conclusions—or do you want both checked?
